summary: Legendary 10/11/83 MSG show captured with excellent sound, highlighted by a blistering St. Stephen, Wang Dang Doong and a powerhouse second‑set Bertha.
review: The October 11, 1983 Madison Square Garden concert is hailed as one of the band’s all‑time greats, with fans pointing to an electrifying St. Stephen, a breakout Wang Dang Doong, and a second‑set that builds into a rousing Bertha. Reviewers praise the recording for its crystal‑clear capture (often recommended as matrix or audience), noting only minor vocal raggedness. Overall the performance’s energy far outweighs any small imperfections.

ai_rating.rationale: Both reviewers gave five stars and emphasized an outstanding show with only minor vocal roughness; recording is described as exquisitely captured, leading to a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as 'exquisitely' captured and highly recommended as matrix or audience; no major technical flaws mentioned.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set packed with high‑energy highlights (Jack Straw, Birdsong, Wang Dang Doong) and a strong second set that ramps up through the pre‑drums run into an especially powerful Bertha.

band_member_comments.Phil: Sang lead on China Doll with his own mic—a rare occurrence noted by reviewers.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drums run described as 'just rockin', emphasizing strong drum energy in the second set.
